title: Themeda quadrivalvis
intro: Themeda quadrivalvis, commonly known as Kangaroo grass, is a species of grass native to Australia and is used in traditional medicine. The health benefits of Themeda quadrivalvis include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Themeda quadrivalvis is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in topical applications, such as salves and creams, to treat skin conditions and wounds.
taxonomy: Themeda quadrivalvis is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Themeda and is often referred to as the black spear grass.
